The surface facts check out: GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md:59 (Step 3 'Update Original Note') instructs calling mcp__graph-brain__save-knowledge with new content; brain-update/SKILL.md:39 and brain-save/SKILL.md:58-64 do the same; brain-history/SKILL.md:27-31 and 81-87 hand-roll history via changelog-note searches and manual restore; and grep across D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain confirms zero occurrences of update-knowledge, get-note-history, or restore-note-version. However, the claim's central harm thesis — that every 'update' likely produces a duplicate note instead of mutating the original — is refuted by the actual server semantics. I loaded the live mcp__graph-brain__save-knowledge schema: its description explicitly says 'prefer update-knowledge (or save-knowledge with the same title — it upserts by title) instead of creating a duplicate', its title parameter says 'If a note with this title already exists, it will be updated (old content saved as version snapshot)', and it has a dedicated reason parameter for upsert version history. Since GRAPH_PROTOCOL Step 3 keeps the original title (only content changes), the upsert mutates the original note — no duplicates, with automatic server-side version snapshots. The changelog notes created in Step 2 have distinct titles ('— Changelog #N') and are intentional separate notes. brain-history's manual restore (changelog + same-title save) is redundant with restore-note-version but functionally correct via the same upsert. The auditor's own hedge ('if save-knowledge upserts by title, document that explicitly') is the case that holds, making this a minor doc/efficiency suggestion rather than the claimed issue.
correction: Not a defect: mcp__graph-brain__save-knowledge upserts by title (confirmed from the live server tool schema, which states 'save-knowledge with the same title — it upserts by title' and 'If a note with this title already exists, it will be updated (old content saved as version snapshot)'). GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md Step 3 and brain-save/brain-update keep the original title when updating, so updates mutate the original note rather than creating duplicates, and the server auto-snapshots prior content. The only valid residue is a low-severity improvement suggestion: (1) GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md could explicitly document the upsert-by-title semantics so the behavior isn't implicit, and (2) brain-update/brain-history could optionally use the dedicated update-knowledge/get-note-history/restore-note-version tools for efficiency — but the current changelog-note approach is an intentional, working design that adds graph-searchable, wiki-linked change records beyond the server's native history.

(6) No setup documentation exists anywhere in the plugin: brain-howto's 'First Time Setup' section (lines 20-24) starts at '/brain-status — check if Graph Brain is connected', assuming the server is already registered; brain-status SKILL.md only hints 'claude mcp list' for diagnostics; the repo root README.md contains zero mentions of 'brain' (case-insensitive grep returned no matches). A fresh marketplace install therefore gets a plugin whose primary functionality cannot work and whose install path for the required MCP server is documented nowhere.
correction: Claim is valid, with two minor precision notes: (a) 'Every skill' is slightly overstated — 11 of 14 skills directly call mcp__graph-brain__ tools; brain-help (command listing), brain-howto (tutorial), and brain-log (reads local .brain/activity-log.json) do not, though they are auxiliary to the MCP-dependent core. (b) 'All fail' overstates the failure mode — the SessionStart hook and skills explicitly handle the disconnected case gracefully (show a Thai warning, never block the session), so the plugin degrades rather than hard-fails. The substantive issue stands: an undeclared, unbundled, undocumented hard dependency on the graph-brain MCP server.
